rawText:
I think and talk a lot about the risks of powerful AI. The company I'm the CEO of, Anthropic, does a lot of research on how to reduce these risks. Because of this, people sometimes draw the conclusion that I'm a pessimist or "doomer" who thinks AI will be mostly bad or dangerous. I don't think that at all. In fact, one of my main reasons for focusing on risks is that they're the only thing standing between us and what I see as a fundamentally positive future. I think that most people are underestimating just how radical the upside of AI could be, just as I think most people are underestimating how bad the risks could be.

In this essay I try to sketch out what that upside might look like—what a world with powerful AI might look like if everything goes right. Of course no one can know the future with any certainty or precision, and the effects of powerful AI are likely to be even more unpredictable than past technological changes, so all of this is unavoidably going to consist of guesses. But I am aiming for at least educated and useful guesses, which capture the flavor of what will happen even if most details end up being wrong. I'm including lots of details mainly because I think a concrete vision does more to advance discussion than a highly hedged and abstract one.

First, however, I wanted to briefly explain why I and Anthropic haven't talked that much about powerful AI's upsides, and why we'll probably continue, overall, to talk a lot about risks. In particular, I've made this choice out of a desire to:

Maximize leverage. The basic development of AI technology and many (not all) of its benefits seems inevitable (unless the risks derail everything) and is fundamentally driven by powerful market forces. On the other hand, the risks are not predetermined and our actions can greatly change their likelihood.

Avoid perception of propaganda. AI companies talking about all the amazing benefits of AI can come off like propagandists, or as if they're attempting to distract from downsides. I also think that as a matter of principle it's bad for your soul to spend too much of your time "talking your book".

Avoid grandiosity. I am often turned off by the way many AI risk public figures (not to mention AI company leaders) talk about the post-AGI world, as if it's their mission to single-handedly bring it about like a prophet leading their people to salvation. I think it's dangerous to view companies as unilaterally shaping the world, and dangerous to view practical technological goals in essentially religious terms.

Yet despite all of the concerns above, I really do think it's important to discuss what a good world with powerful AI could look like, while doing our best to avoid the above pitfalls. In fact I think it is critical to have a genuinely inspiring vision of the future, and not just a plan to fight fires. Many of the implications of powerful AI are adversarial or dangerous, but at the end of it all, there has to be something we're fighting for, some positive-sum outcome where everyone is better off, something to rally people to rise above their squabbles and confront the challenges ahead. Fear is one kind of motivator, but it's not enough: we need hope as well.

sentences.original: I think and talk a lot about the risks of powerful AI.
sentences.translation: 저는 강력한 AI의 위험성에 대해 많이 생각하고 이야기합니다.

grammarElements.text: about the risks of powerful AI
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (주제)

vocabulary.example: The CEO leads a powerful technology company.
There are significant risks associated with the new technology.

expressions.phrase: think and talk a lot about
expressions.usage: 자신의 주요 관심사를 소개할 때

patterns.pattern: I [verb A] and [verb B] a lot about [topic]
patterns.explanation: 두 개의 동사를 병렬로 연결하여 자신의 관심사를 소개하는 패턴. 에세이 도입부에서 자주 쓰인다.
examples: I read and write a lot about climate change.
I think and worry a lot about the future of education.

drillQuestions.question: I think and talk a lot _____ the risks of AI.
I think and _____ a lot about AI risks.

sentences.notes: 에세이의 첫 문장으로, 저자가 AI 위험에 대한 전문가임을 짧고 명료하게 밝힌다. "think and talk"의 병렬 구조가 지적 활동과 공적 담론 모두를 포괄한다.

koreanAlignment.en: I think and talk a lot
about the risks of powerful AI

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 에토스(ethos) 확립 — 첫 문장에서 자신이 AI 위험 전문가임을 밝혀 신뢰성 구축

sentences.original: The company I'm the CEO of, Anthropic, does a lot of research on how to reduce these risks.
sentences.translation: 제가 CEO로 있는 회사, Anthropic은 이러한 위험을 줄이는 방법에 대해 많은 연구를 수행합니다.

grammarElements.text: The company I'm the CEO of
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계대명사 목적격 생략 + 전치사 잔류)

grammarElements.text: on how to reduce these risks
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (주제)

vocabulary.example: She became the CEO of a Fortune 500 company.
The new policy aims to reduce carbon emissions.

expressions.phrase: the company I'm the CEO of
expressions.usage: 관계대명사를 생략하고 전치사가 문장 끝에 남는 구어적 구조
연구 활동을 설명할 때. conduct research보다 구어적

patterns.pattern: The [noun] I'm the [title] of, [name], [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: 자신의 직위와 소속 조직을 자연스럽게 소개하는 패턴. 관계대명사 생략과 동격이 결합된 구조.
examples: The team I'm the leader of, Project Alpha, is working on this.
The department I'm the head of, Research, has 50 members.

drillQuestions.question: The company I'm the CEO _____, Anthropic, does a lot of research.
Anthropic does a lot of research _____ how to reduce risks.

sentences.notes: "The company I'm the CEO of"에서 관계대명사(that/which)가 생략되고 전치사 of가 문장 끝에 남아 있다. 격식체에서는 "The company of which I am the CEO"가 되지만, 이 에세이의 친근한 문체에 맞게 구어적 형태를 사용한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The company I'm the CEO of
Anthropic
does a lot of research
on how to reduce these risks

sentences.original: Because of this, people sometimes draw the conclusion that I'm a pessimist or "doomer" who thinks AI will be mostly bad or dangerous.
sentences.translation: 이 때문에, 사람들은 때때로 제가 AI가 대부분 나쁘거나 위험할 것이라고 생각하는 비관론자 또는 "파멸주의자"라는 결론을 내립니다.

grammarElements.text: who thinks AI will be mostly bad or dangerous
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (who — pessimist/doomer를 수식)

vocabulary.example: Don't draw conclusions too quickly.
He's a pessimist who always expects the worst.
Some call him a doomer because he warns about AI risks.

expressions.usage: 앞서 언급한 내용을 원인으로 제시할 때
사람들이 (잘못된) 결론에 도달하는 과정을 묘사

patterns.pattern: Because of [X], people sometimes draw the conclusion that ...
patterns.explanation: 다른 사람들의 오해를 설명하는 패턴. 자신의 입장을 정정하기 전에 사용.
examples: Because of his silence, people drew the conclusion that he agreed.
Because of the data, researchers drew the conclusion that the drug was effective.

drillQuestions.question: People sometimes draw the _____ that I'm a pessimist.
_____ of this, people sometimes misunderstand me.

sentences.notes: "doomer"는 따옴표로 감싸져 있어 일상 용어가 아닌 특정 커뮤니티의 레이블임을 암시한다. AI 안전 분야에서 위험을 지나치게 강조한다고 비판받는 사람을 가리키는 비격식 용어이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Because of this
people sometimes draw the conclusion
that I'm a pessimist or "doomer"
who thinks AI will be mostly bad or dangerous
koreanAlignment.ko: AI가 대부분 나쁘거나 위험할 것이라고 생각하는

sentences.original: I don't think that at all.
sentences.translation: 저는 전혀 그렇게 생각하지 않습니다.

vocabulary.example: I don't agree with that at all.

expressions.phrase: I don't think that at all
expressions.usage: 앞서 제기된 오해를 단호하게 부정할 때

patterns.pattern: I don't [verb] that at all.
patterns.explanation: 앞에서 언급된 관점을 전면 부정하는 짧고 강력한 패턴.
examples: I don't believe that at all.
I don't accept that at all.

drillQuestions.question: I don't think that _____ all.

sentences.notes: 의도적으로 짧은 문장. 앞의 긴 문장에서 묘사한 오해를 간결하고 단호하게 부정한다. "at all"이 부정문을 강조하여 "전혀"라는 의미를 더한다.

koreanAlignment.en: I don't think that
at all

pronunciationNotes: at all: /ət ɔːl/ — 빠르게 발음하면 "애톨"처럼 연음
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 단호한 부정(emphatic denial) — 긴 문장 뒤 의도적으로 짧은 문장으로 논점을 명확히

sentences.original: In fact, one of my main reasons for focusing on risks is that they're the only thing standing between us and what I see as a fundamentally positive future.
sentences.translation: 사실, 제가 위험에 집중하는 주된 이유 중 하나는, 위험이 우리와 제가 근본적으로 긍정적이라고 보는 미래 사이에 놓인 유일한 장애물이기 때문입니다.

grammarElements.text: one of my main reasons for focusing on risks
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: that they're the only thing standing between us and what I see as a fundamentally positive future
grammarElements.label: that절 (보어)

vocabulary.example: The two approaches are fundamentally different.
Nothing is standing between you and your goals.

expressions.phrase: one of my main reasons for
expressions.usage: 여러 이유 중 핵심적인 것을 제시할 때
개인적 관점이나 해석을 제시할 때

patterns.pattern: One of my main reasons for [gerund] is that ...
patterns.explanation: 자신의 행동이나 선택의 이유를 격식체로 설명하는 패턴.
examples: One of my main reasons for studying abroad is that I want cultural exposure.
One of my main reasons for leaving was that I needed a change.

drillQuestions.question: One of my main reasons _____ focusing on risks is that...
They're the only thing _____ between us and a positive future.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 에세이의 핵심 역설(paradox)을 담고 있다: 위험에 집중하는 사람이 사실은 긍정적 미래를 가장 강하게 믿는다. "standing between us and X"는 은유적 표현으로 위험이 장애물임을 나타낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: In fact
one of my main reasons for focusing on risks
koreanAlignment.ko: 위험에 집중하는 주된 이유 중 하나는

koreanAlignment.en: is that
they're the only thing standing between us
koreanAlignment.ko: 위험이 우리 사이에 놓인 유일한 장애물

koreanAlignment.en: and what I see as a fundamentally positive future
koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 제가 근본적으로 긍정적이라고 보는 미래

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 역설(paradox) — 위험 전문가가 사실은 낙관론자임을 밝히는 반전

sentences.original: I think that most people are underestimating just how radical the upside of AI could be, just as I think most people are underestimating how bad the risks could be.
sentences.translation: 대부분의 사람들이 AI의 긍정적 가능성이 얼마나 급진적일 수 있는지 과소평가하고 있다고 생각합니다. 마치 대부분의 사람들이 위험이 얼마나 심각할 수 있는지 과소평가하는 것과 마찬가지로요.

grammarElements.text: just how radical the upside of AI could be
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (간접의문문)

grammarElements.text: just as I think most people are underestimating how bad the risks could be
grammarElements.label: 비교절 (just as)

vocabulary.example: People often underestimate the difficulty of learning a new language.
The company made radical changes to its business model.
The upside of the new technology is enormous.

expressions.usage: just를 how 앞에 두어 정도를 강조
두 가지 유사한 상황을 대비할 때

patterns.pattern: Most people are underestimating [how + adjective + subject + could be], just as [parallel clause]
patterns.explanation: 두 가지 과소평가를 대구법으로 병렬 배치하여 균형 잡힌 시각을 보여주는 패턴.
examples: People are underestimating how powerful AI could be, just as they underestimate how dangerous it could be.

drillQuestions.question: Most people are _____ just how radical AI could be.
The upside could be radical, just _____ the risks could be bad.

sentences.notes: "just as"로 시작하는 병렬 구조가 핵심이다. 긍정적 가능성의 과소평가와 위험의 과소평가를 동시에 지적함으로써, 저자가 단순한 낙관론자도 비관론자도 아닌 균형 잡힌 시각을 가졌음을 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: I think that most people are underestimating
just how radical the upside of AI could be
koreanAlignment.ko: AI의 긍정적 가능성이 얼마나 급진적일 수 있는지

koreanAlignment.en: just as I think most people are underestimating
how bad the risks could be

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대구법(parallelism) — "just how radical ... just as ... how bad"로 긍정과 부정 양면을 균형 있게 제시

sentences.original: In this essay I try to sketch out what that upside might look like—what a world with powerful AI might look like if everything goes right.
sentences.translation: 이 에세이에서 저는 그 긍정적 가능성이 어떤 모습일지를 그려보려 합니다 — 만약 모든 것이 잘 된다면 강력한 AI가 있는 세상이 어떤 모습일지를요.

grammarElements.text: what that upside might look like
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (간접의문문)

grammarElements.text: what a world with powerful AI might look like if everything goes right
grammarElements.label: 동격 (em dash 뒤 부연설명)

vocabulary.example: Let me sketch out the plan for you.
There's a huge upside to investing early.

expressions.usage: 가능한 미래나 시나리오를 탐구할 때
최선의 시나리오를 가정할 때

patterns.pattern: In this [text type] I try to sketch out what [X] might look like
patterns.explanation: 에세이나 글의 목적을 밝히는 메타 서술 패턴. 학술적 글쓰기의 전형적인 도입부.
examples: In this report I try to sketch out what the market might look like in 2030.
In this chapter I try to sketch out what a solution might look like.

drillQuestions.question: I try to sketch _____ what the future might look like.
What might the world look like _____ everything goes right?

sentences.notes: em dash(—)로 앞의 내용을 부연 설명하는 구조. "sketch out"은 세부사항 없이 큰 그림을 그린다는 의미로, 저자가 예측의 불확실성을 인정하는 겸손한 어조를 반영한다.

koreanAlignment.en: In this essay
I try to sketch out
what that upside might look like
what a world with powerful AI might look like
koreanAlignment.ko: 강력한 AI가 있는 세상이 어떤 모습일지

koreanAlignment.en: if everything goes right

sentences.original: Of course no one can know the future with any certainty or precision, and the effects of powerful AI are likely to be even more unpredictable than past technological changes, so all of this is unavoidably going to consist of guesses.
sentences.translation: 물론 아무도 미래를 확실하게 또는 정밀하게 알 수 없으며, 강력한 AI의 효과는 과거의 기술 변화보다 훨씬 더 예측 불가능할 것이므로, 이 모든 것은 불가피하게 추측으로 이루어질 수밖에 없습니다.

grammarElements.text: no one can know the future with any certainty or precision
grammarElements.label: 주절 1

grammarElements.text: and the effects of powerful AI are likely to be even more unpredictable than past technological changes
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (and)

grammarElements.text: so all of this is unavoidably going to consist of guesses
grammarElements.label: 결과절 (so)

vocabulary.example: There is no certainty about the outcome.
The instrument measures with great precision.
The weather in spring is highly unpredictable.
The project was unavoidably delayed.

expressions.usage: 지식의 한계를 인정할 때
예측의 불확실성을 솔직하게 인정

patterns.pattern: Of course no one can [verb] with any [noun], and [X] is likely to be even more [adjective] than [Y], so ...
patterns.explanation: 겸손한 양보(of course) → 불확실성 강조(even more) → 결론(so)으로 이어지는 논리적 서술 패턴.
examples: Of course no one can predict markets with any accuracy, and this year is likely to be even more volatile than last, so we must remain cautious.

drillQuestions.question: No one can know the future with any certainty _____ precision.
The effects are likely to be even more _____ than past changes.

sentences.notes: 세 개의 절이 and와 so로 연결된 복합문. un- 접두사 단어가 두 개(unpredictable, unavoidably) 등장하며, 예측의 한계를 정직하게 인정하는 학술적 글쓰기의 관습을 따른다.

koreanAlignment.en: Of course no one can know the future
koreanAlignment.ko: 물론 아무도 미래를 알 수 없으며

koreanAlignment.en: with any certainty or precision
the effects of powerful AI are likely to be
even more unpredictable than past technological changes
koreanAlignment.ko: 과거의 기술 변화보다 훨씬 더 예측 불가능할

koreanAlignment.en: so all of this is unavoidably going to consist of guesses
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 모든 것은 불가피하게 추측으로 이루어질 수밖에 없습니다

sentences.original: But I am aiming for at least educated and useful guesses, which capture the flavor of what will happen even if most details end up being wrong.
sentences.translation: 하지만 저는 최소한 지적이고 유용한 추측을 목표로 하고 있으며, 대부분의 세부사항이 결국 틀리더라도 실제 일어날 일의 본질을 포착하는 추측을 하려 합니다.

grammarElements.text: at least educated and useful guesses
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: which capture the flavor of what will happen
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (which — 계속적 용법)

grammarElements.text: even if most details end up being wrong
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (even if)

vocabulary.example: Based on the data, we can make an educated guess.
The documentary captures the flavor of life in the 1960s.
The project ended up being more expensive than expected.

expressions.usage: 겸손한 목표를 제시하면서도 가치를 주장할 때

expressions.phrase: even if most details end up being wrong
expressions.usage: 핵심 통찰의 가치를 방어하면서 세부 오류를 인정

patterns.pattern: I am aiming for [X], which [verb] ... even if [Y]
patterns.explanation: 목표를 제시하고, 관계대명사로 그 가치를 설명하며, even if로 한계를 인정하는 다층 구조.
examples: I am aiming for a rough estimate, which gives us direction even if the numbers are off.

drillQuestions.question: I am aiming _____ at least educated guesses.
Even if most details end _____ being wrong, the guesses are useful.

sentences.notes: "educated guess"는 근거에 기반한 추측을 뜻하는 관용 표현. "capture the flavor"는 음식에서 유래한 은유로, 전체적인 느낌이나 본질을 파악한다는 의미이다.

koreanAlignment.en: But I am aiming for
at least educated and useful guesses
which capture the flavor of what will happen
even if most details end up being wrong

sentences.original: I'm including lots of details mainly because I think a concrete vision does more to advance discussion than a highly hedged and abstract one.
sentences.translation: 많은 세부사항을 포함하는 이유는 주로, 구체적인 비전이 지나치게 조심스럽고 추상적인 것보다 논의를 진전시키는 데 더 효과적이라고 생각하기 때문입니다.

grammarElements.text: a concrete vision does more to advance discussion
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (비교)

grammarElements.text: than a highly hedged and abstract one
grammarElements.label: 비교절 (than)

vocabulary.example: We need concrete evidence to support the theory.
His hedged response suggested he wasn't fully confident.
The concept is too abstract for most people to understand.
The research advanced our understanding of the disease.

expressions.usage: 여러 이유 중 가장 중요한 것을 지목
비교급 구조로 효과의 차이를 설명

patterns.pattern: I'm [verb]-ing [X] mainly because [a concrete Y] does more to [verb] than [a hedged/abstract Z]
patterns.explanation: 자신의 글쓰기 선택을 정당화하는 메타 서술 패턴. 구체성의 가치를 주장.
examples: I'm using real examples mainly because concrete cases do more to teach than abstract theories.

drillQuestions.question: A concrete vision does more to _____ discussion.
I'm including details _____ because I think it helps.

sentences.notes: "hedged"는 금융의 "헤지(위험 회피)"에서 유래하여, 단언을 피하고 조건이나 단서를 다는 것을 의미. "a highly hedged and abstract one"에서 one은 vision을 대신하는 대명사.

koreanAlignment.en: I'm including lots of details
mainly because I think
a concrete vision does more
to advance discussion
than a highly hedged and abstract one

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 메타 서술(meta-commentary) — 자신의 글쓰기 방법론을 독자에게 설명하며 투명성을 보여줌

sentences.original: First, however, I wanted to briefly explain why I and Anthropic haven't talked that much about powerful AI's upsides, and why we'll probably continue, overall, to talk a lot about risks.
sentences.translation: 그러나 먼저, 저와 Anthropic이 왜 강력한 AI의 긍정적 측면에 대해 그다지 많이 이야기하지 않았는지, 그리고 왜 전반적으로 위험에 대해 계속 많이 이야기할 것인지를 간단히 설명하고 싶습니다.

grammarElements.text: why I and Anthropic haven't talked that much about powerful AI's upsides
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (간접의문문 1)

grammarElements.text: and why we'll probably continue, overall, to talk a lot about risks
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (간접의문문 2)

vocabulary.example: Let me briefly explain the main points.
Overall, the project was a success.

expressions.usage: 주제를 전환하기 전에 선행 설명이 필요할 때
의도적 침묵의 이유를 설명하기 전에

patterns.pattern: First, however, I wanted to briefly explain why [clause A], and why [clause B]
patterns.explanation: 본론에 들어가기 전 맥락을 설명하는 학술적 전환 패턴. 두 개의 why절이 병렬.
examples: First, however, I wanted to explain why we changed our approach, and why we believe it's the right one.

drillQuestions.question: I wanted to briefly explain _____ we haven't talked about the upsides.
We'll probably _____, overall, to talk about risks.

sentences.notes: "first, however"는 "먼저 말해둘 것이 있다"는 뜻으로, 본론(AI의 긍정적 미래)에 들어가기 전 우회적 설명을 예고한다. "I and Anthropic"에서 격식 문법상 "Anthropic and I"가 더 전통적이지만, 저자가 개인적 관점을 먼저 내세운다.

koreanAlignment.en: First, however
I wanted to briefly explain
why I and Anthropic haven't talked that much
koreanAlignment.ko: 왜 저와 Anthropic이 그다지 많이 이야기하지 않았는지

koreanAlignment.en: about powerful AI's upsides
and why we'll probably continue, overall, to talk a lot about risks
koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 왜 전반적으로 위험에 대해 많이 이야기할 것인지

sentences.original: The basic development of AI technology and many (not all) of its benefits seems inevitable (unless the risks derail everything) and is fundamentally driven by powerful market forces.
sentences.translation: AI 기술의 기본적인 발전과 그 혜택의 많은 부분(전부는 아니지만)은 불가피해 보이며(위험이 모든 것을 탈선시키지 않는 한), 근본적으로 강력한 시장 논리에 의해 추동됩니다.

grammarElements.text: The basic development of AI technology and many (not all) of its benefits
grammarElements.label: 주어 (복합 — 두 명사구)

grammarElements.text: and is fundamentally driven by powerful market forces
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (수동태)

vocabulary.example: Change is inevitable in any growing organization.
The scandal could derail the entire project.
Market forces determine the price of goods.

expressions.usage: 괄호 안에서 앞 진술을 한정하는 삽입 표현

expressions.meaning: 위험이 모든 것을 좌절시키지 않는 한
expressions.usage: 예외적 시나리오를 단서로 삽입

patterns.pattern: [X] seems inevitable (unless [condition]) and is fundamentally driven by [Y]
patterns.explanation: 불가피성을 주장하면서 예외 조건을 삽입하고, 원동력을 밝히는 분석적 패턴.
examples: Automation seems inevitable (unless regulation blocks it) and is fundamentally driven by cost reduction.

drillQuestions.question: The development seems _____ and is driven by market forces.
Unless the risks _____ everything, progress will continue.

sentences.notes: 괄호가 두 번 사용되어 본문의 흐름을 유지하면서 중요한 단서를 삽입한다. "derail"은 기차 탈선에서 유래한 은유로, 계획이나 진행이 갑자기 중단되는 것을 의미.

koreanAlignment.en: The basic development of AI technology
and many (not all) of its benefits
seems inevitable
(unless the risks derail everything)
and is fundamentally driven by powerful market forces
koreanAlignment.ko: 근본적으로 강력한 시장 논리에 의해 추동됩니다

sentences.original: On the other hand, the risks are not predetermined and our actions can greatly change their likelihood.
sentences.translation: 반면에, 위험은 미리 정해진 것이 아니며, 우리의 행동이 그 가능성을 크게 바꿀 수 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: and our actions can greatly change their likelihood
grammarElements.label: 등위절

vocabulary.example: The outcome was not predetermined.
The likelihood of success is high.

expressions.usage: 앞의 내용과 대조되는 관점을 제시
영향력의 크기를 강조

patterns.pattern: On the other hand, [X] is not predetermined and [Y] can greatly change [Z]
patterns.explanation: 앞 문장과 대비하여 인간의 영향력과 행위성(agency)을 주장하는 패턴.
examples: On the other hand, the future is not predetermined and our choices can greatly change the outcome.

drillQuestions.question: On the _____ hand, the risks are not predetermined.
Our actions can greatly change their _____.

sentences.notes: "predetermined"는 pre-(미리) + determined(결정된)의 합성어. 이 문장은 저자의 핵심 논리를 담고 있다: AI의 혜택은 불가피하지만, 위험은 우리가 통제할 수 있으므로 위험에 집중하는 것이 전략적으로 합리적이다.

koreanAlignment.en: On the other hand
the risks are not predetermined
and our actions can greatly change
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리의 행동이 크게 바꿀 수 있습니다

koreanAlignment.en: their likelihood

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대조(contrast) — "inevitable"과 "not predetermined"을 병치하여 행위성(agency)의 논거를 세움

sentences.original: AI companies talking about all the amazing benefits of AI can come off like propagandists, or as if they're attempting to distract from downsides.
sentences.translation: AI 회사들이 AI의 놀라운 혜택에 대해 이야기하면 선전가처럼 보이거나, 단점으로부터 주의를 돌리려는 것처럼 비칠 수 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: AI companies talking about all the amazing benefits of AI
grammarElements.label: 주어 (현재분사구 후치수식)

grammarElements.text: or as if they're attempting to distract from downsides
grammarElements.label: 양태절 (as if)

vocabulary.example: He came off as arrogant in the interview.
Critics accused the media of acting like propagandists.
The scandal distracted from the real issues.
Every technology has its downsides.

expressions.usage: 타인에게 주는 인상을 묘사할 때. 대개 부정적 인상
타인의 의도에 대한 인식을 묘사

patterns.pattern: [Subject] talking about [X] can come off like [Y], or as if ...
patterns.explanation: 특정 행동이 외부에 어떻게 인식되는지를 경고하는 패턴.
examples: Politicians talking about their achievements can come off like braggarts, or as if they're ignoring problems.

drillQuestions.question: AI companies talking about benefits can come _____ like propagandists.
They seem as if they're attempting to _____ from downsides.

sentences.notes: "come off like propagandists"는 강한 표현으로, AI 기업들의 자기 홍보에 대한 대중의 불신을 직시한다. 저자가 자기 회사(Anthropic)를 포함한 AI 기업의 입장에서 솔직한 자기비판을 하고 있다.

koreanAlignment.en: AI companies talking about all the amazing benefits of AI
koreanAlignment.ko: AI 회사들이 AI의 놀라운 혜택에 대해 이야기하면

koreanAlignment.en: can come off like propagandists
or as if they're attempting to distract from downsides

sentences.original: I also think that as a matter of principle it's bad for your soul to spend too much of your time "talking your book".
sentences.translation: 또한 원칙적으로, 너무 많은 시간을 "자기 책을 이야기하는 것(자기 이해에 맞게 말하는 것)"에 쓰는 것은 영혼에 좋지 않다고 생각합니다.

grammarElements.text: to spend too much of your time "talking your book"
grammarElements.label: 진주어 (to부정사)

vocabulary.example: As a matter of principle, I never accept gifts from clients.
Analysts are often accused of talking their book.
Music is good for the soul.

expressions.usage: 어떤 행위가 인격적으로 해롭다고 비유적으로 표현
금융 용어에서 유래. 자신의 포지션에 유리한 주장을 하는 것

patterns.pattern: As a matter of principle, it's [adjective] for [someone] to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 원칙에 기반한 도덕적 판단을 표현하는 패턴. 가주어 it 구문.
examples: As a matter of principle, it's wrong for leaders to deceive their people.
As a matter of principle, it's important for scientists to share their data.

drillQuestions.question: As a matter _____ principle, it's bad for your soul.
It's bad for your soul to spend too _____ time talking your book.

sentences.notes: "talking your book"은 월스트리트 금융 용어로, 투자자가 자신의 포지션(보유 주식 등)에 유리한 방향으로 공개 발언하는 것을 뜻한다. Dario Amodei는 AI 회사 CEO로서 AI의 이점을 과도하게 홍보하는 것이 이와 같다고 비유한다.

koreanAlignment.en: I also think that
as a matter of principle
it's bad for your soul
to spend too much of your time
"talking your book"

pronunciationNotes: principle: /ˈprɪnsɪpl/ — "프린시플", principal(교장)과 발음 동일하지만 의미 다름
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 금융 은유(financial metaphor) — "talking your book"이라는 업계 용어로 자기이해 편향을 비판

sentences.original: I am often turned off by the way many AI risk public figures (not to mention AI company leaders) talk about the post-AGI world, as if it's their mission to single-handedly bring it about like a prophet leading their people to salvation.
sentences.translation: 많은 AI 위험 분야 공적 인물들이(AI 회사 리더들은 말할 것도 없고) 포스트-AGI 세계에 대해 이야기하는 방식에 저는 종종 거부감을 느낍니다. 마치 선지자가 사람들을 구원으로 이끄는 것처럼, 혼자서 그 세계를 실현하는 것이 자신의 사명인 양 말입니다.

grammarElements.text: many AI risk public figures (not to mention AI company leaders) talk about the post-AGI world
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (way를 수식)

grammarElements.text: as if it's their mission to single-handedly bring it about
grammarElements.label: 양태절 (as if)

grammarElements.text: like a prophet leading their people to salvation
grammarElements.label: 비유구 (like)

vocabulary.example: I was turned off by his arrogant attitude.
The food was expensive, not to mention tasteless.
She single-handedly built the business from scratch.
Many saw technology as the salvation of humanity.
He spoke like a prophet warning of danger.

expressions.usage: 특정 행동이나 말투에 대한 부정적 반응
어떤 상태나 결과를 만들어내는 것

patterns.pattern: I am often turned off by the way [people] [verb], as if it's their mission to [verb] like [metaphor]
patterns.explanation: 타인의 태도에 대한 불편함을 표현하고, as if와 like로 과장된 비유를 이중으로 사용하는 패턴.
examples: I am often turned off by the way some experts talk, as if it's their mission to enlighten everyone like a guru teaching the masses.

drillQuestions.question: I am often turned _____ by the way they talk.
As if it's their mission to _____ bring it about.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 실리콘밸리의 메시아 콤플렉스에 대한 날카로운 비판이다. "prophet leading their people to salvation"이라는 종교적 비유가 기술 낙관론자들의 과대망상적 자기 인식을 풍자한다. post-AGI는 "범용 인공지능(AGI) 이후"를 뜻하는 AI 업계 용어.

koreanAlignment.en: I am often turned off
by the way many AI risk public figures
koreanAlignment.ko: 많은 AI 위험 분야 공적 인물들이

koreanAlignment.en: (not to mention AI company leaders)
talk about the post-AGI world
as if it's their mission to single-handedly bring it about
koreanAlignment.ko: 마치 혼자서 그것을 실현하는 것이 자신의 사명인 양

koreanAlignment.en: like a prophet leading their people to salvation

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 종교적 비유(religious analogy) — "prophet"과 "salvation"으로 기술 리더들의 메시아 콤플렉스를 풍자

sentences.original: I think it's dangerous to view companies as unilaterally shaping the world, and dangerous to view practical technological goals in essentially religious terms.
sentences.translation: 회사들이 일방적으로 세계를 형성한다고 보는 것은 위험하며, 실용적인 기술 목표를 본질적으로 종교적 용어로 보는 것도 위험하다고 생각합니다.

grammarElements.text: it's dangerous to view companies as unilaterally shaping the world
grammarElements.label: 가주어 구문 1

grammarElements.text: and dangerous to view practical technological goals in essentially religious terms
grammarElements.label: 병렬 가주어 구문 2 (it 생략)

vocabulary.example: The country acted unilaterally without consulting allies.
The two proposals are essentially the same.
Let me explain it in simple terms.

expressions.usage: 관점이나 해석을 표현할 때
비종교적 대상에 종교적 프레임을 적용하는 것을 비판할 때

patterns.pattern: It's dangerous to view [X] as [verb]-ing, and dangerous to view [Y] in [Z] terms
patterns.explanation: 동일한 구조("dangerous to view")를 반복하여 두 가지 위험한 관점을 병렬로 경고하는 패턴.
examples: It's dangerous to view success as permanent, and dangerous to view failure in absolute terms.

drillQuestions.question: It's dangerous to view companies _____ unilaterally shaping the world.
It's dangerous to view technological goals in religious _____.

sentences.notes: "dangerous"가 두 번 반복되어 병렬 구조를 형성한다. 두 번째 절에서 it이 생략되었다. "religious terms"는 AI 발전을 종교적 사명으로 보는 실리콘밸리 문화에 대한 비판이다.

koreanAlignment.en: I think it's dangerous to view
companies as unilaterally shaping the world
and dangerous to view
practical technological goals
in essentially religious terms

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 반복(anaphora) — "dangerous to view"를 반복하여 경고의 강도를 높임

sentences.original: Yet despite all of the concerns above, I really do think it's important to discuss what a good world with powerful AI could look like, while doing our best to avoid the above pitfalls.
sentences.translation: 그럼에도 불구하고 위에서 언급한 모든 우려에도 불구하고, 위의 함정들을 최선을 다해 피하면서 강력한 AI가 있는 좋은 세상이 어떤 모습일 수 있는지 논의하는 것이 정말 중요하다고 생각합니다.

grammarElements.text: Yet despite all of the concerns above
grammarElements.label: 양보 부사구

grammarElements.text: it's important to discuss what a good world with powerful AI could look like
grammarElements.label: 가주어 구문 + 간접의문문

grammarElements.text: while doing our best to avoid the above pitfalls
grammarElements.label: 양보/동시 부사절

vocabulary.example: There are many pitfalls in starting a new business.
Safety is a major concern for parents.

expressions.usage: 이전의 반론들을 인정한 뒤 자신의 주장으로 돌아올 때
do를 삽입하여 확신을 강조
노력의 의지를 표현하면서 완벽은 보장하지 않는 겸손한 표현

patterns.pattern: Yet despite all of [concerns], I really do think it's important to [verb], while doing our best to avoid [pitfalls]
patterns.explanation: 이전 반론을 모두 인정한 뒤, 강조 do와 함께 본래 주장으로 복귀하는 전환 패턴.
examples: Yet despite all of the obstacles, I really do think it's important to try, while doing our best to avoid past mistakes.

drillQuestions.question: Yet _____ all of the concerns, I think this is important.
I really _____ think it's important to discuss this.

sentences.notes: "I really do think"에서 do는 강조(emphatic do)로, 앞에서 여러 단서와 반론을 제시한 후 자신의 핵심 주장을 확고히 되살리는 역할을 한다. 이 문장은 에세이의 구조적 전환점이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Yet despite all of the concerns above
I really do think
it's important to discuss
what a good world with powerful AI could look like
koreanAlignment.ko: 강력한 AI가 있는 좋은 세상이 어떤 모습일 수 있는지

koreanAlignment.en: while doing our best to avoid the above pitfalls

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 양보 후 강조(concession + emphatic assertion) — 반론을 모두 수용한 뒤 do 강조로 핵심 주장을 되살림

sentences.original: In fact I think it is critical to have a genuinely inspiring vision of the future, and not just a plan to fight fires.
sentences.translation: 사실 진정으로 영감을 주는 미래 비전을 갖는 것이 매우 중요하며, 단순히 불을 끄는 계획만으로는 부족하다고 생각합니다.

grammarElements.text: it is critical to have a genuinely inspiring vision of the future
grammarElements.label: 가주어 구문

grammarElements.text: and not just a plan to fight fires
grammarElements.label: 대조 등위절 (not just)

vocabulary.example: It is critical that we act now.
She was genuinely surprised by the news.
We spend too much time fighting fires instead of planning ahead.

expressions.phrase: not just a plan to fight fires
expressions.meaning: 단순히 급한 불을 끄는 계획이 아닌
expressions.usage: 반응적 대응을 넘어 능동적 비전이 필요함을 주장
피상적이지 않은 진정한 영감의 원천을 강조

patterns.pattern: It is critical to have [positive vision], and not just [reactive measure]
patterns.explanation: 방어적 대응의 한계를 지적하고 긍정적 비전의 필요성을 주장하는 패턴.
examples: It is critical to have a long-term strategy, and not just a response to the current crisis.

drillQuestions.question: It is _____ to have an inspiring vision of the future.
We need a vision, not just a plan to fight _____.

sentences.notes: "fight fires"는 소방관이 불을 끄는 것에서 유래한 관용 표현으로, 근본적 해결 없이 발생하는 문제에만 대응하는 것을 뜻한다. 저자는 위험 관리만으로는 부족하며, 영감을 주는 긍정적 비전이 필요하다고 주장한다.

koreanAlignment.en: In fact I think
it is critical to have
a genuinely inspiring vision of the future
and not just a plan to fight fires

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 은유(metaphor) — "fight fires"라는 소방 은유로 반응적 대응의 한계를 비판

sentences.original: Many of the implications of powerful AI are adversarial or dangerous, but at the end of it all, there has to be something we're fighting for, some positive-sum outcome where everyone is better off, something to rally people to rise above their squabbles and confront the challenges ahead.
sentences.translation: 강력한 AI의 함의 중 많은 것이 적대적이거나 위험하지만, 결국에는 우리가 싸우고 있는 대상이 있어야 합니다 — 모두가 더 나아지는 포지티브섬(양의 합) 결과, 사람들이 다툼을 넘어 앞으로의 도전에 맞서도록 결집시키는 무언가 말입니다.

grammarElements.text: Many of the implications of powerful AI are adversarial or dangerous
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (주절 1)

grammarElements.text: but at the end of it all
grammarElements.label: 전환 부사구

grammarElements.text: there has to be something we're fighting for
grammarElements.label: 존재 구문 + 관계대명사절

grammarElements.text: some positive-sum outcome where everyone is better off
grammarElements.label: 동격 1 + 관계부사절

grammarElements.text: something to rally people to rise above their squabbles and confront the challenges ahead
grammarElements.label: 동격 2 + to부정사구

vocabulary.example: The relationship between the two countries became adversarial.
Trade is often described as a positive-sum game.
The leader rallied the troops before the battle.
The siblings had a squabble over the toy.
We must confront the challenges ahead.

expressions.phrase: at the end of it all
expressions.usage: 최종적인 결론이나 핵심 요점으로 돌아올 때

expressions.meaning: 우리가 싸우고 있는 대상, 추구하는 것
expressions.usage: 목적의식과 동기를 강조
사소한 갈등을 초월하여 더 큰 목표에 집중할 것을 촉구

patterns.pattern: There has to be something we're fighting for, some [positive outcome], something to [verb] people to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 세 번의 "something/some"으로 긍정적 비전을 점층적으로 구체화하는 수사적 병렬 구조.
examples: There has to be a reason we're here, some higher purpose, something to give meaning to our efforts.

drillQuestions.question: At the end of it all, there _____ to be something worth fighting for.
Something to rally people to rise _____ their squabbles.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 에세이에서 가장 웅변적인 구절 중 하나이다. "something ... some ... something"의 세 겹 반복이 점층적으로 비전을 구체화한다. "positive-sum"은 게임이론 용어로, 한쪽이 이기면 다른 쪽이 지는 "zero-sum"과 반대 개념.

koreanAlignment.en: Many of the implications of powerful AI are adversarial or dangerous
koreanAlignment.ko: 강력한 AI의 함의 중 많은 것이 적대적이거나 위험하지만

koreanAlignment.en: but at the end of it all
there has to be something we're fighting for
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리가 싸우고 있는 대상이 있어야 합니다

koreanAlignment.en: some positive-sum outcome where everyone is better off
koreanAlignment.ko: 모두가 더 나아지는 양의 합 결과

koreanAlignment.en: something to rally people to rise above their squabbles
and confront the challenges ahead

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 점층법(climax) + 삼중 반복(tricolon) — "something ... some ... something"으로 비전을 점점 구체화

sentences.original: Fear is one kind of motivator, but it's not enough: we need hope as well.
sentences.translation: 두려움은 하나의 동기부여이지만, 그것만으로는 충분하지 않습니다: 우리에게는 희망도 필요합니다.

vocabulary.example: Money is a powerful motivator for many people.
She speaks French and German as well.

expressions.usage: 현재 상태의 한계를 지적하고 추가 요소를 요구
두려움 중심 담론에서 희망의 필요성으로 전환

patterns.pattern: [X] is one kind of [noun], but it's not enough: we need [Y] as well.
patterns.explanation: 한 가지 요소를 인정하되 부족함을 지적하고, 콜론 뒤에 보완적 요소를 제시하는 패턴.
examples: Discipline is one kind of motivator, but it's not enough: we need passion as well.
Data is one kind of evidence, but it's not enough: we need context as well.

drillQuestions.question: Fear is one kind of motivator, but it's not _____.
We need hope as _____.

sentences.notes: 에세이 도입부의 마지막 문장이자 가장 기억에 남는 구절. "Fear ... hope"의 대비는 인류 역사에서 반복되는 보편적 주제이다. 콜론(:)은 앞 내용의 결론이나 설명을 이끌어낸다. 이 문장은 에세이 전체의 존재 이유를 한 줄로 요약한다.

koreanAlignment.en: Fear is one kind of motivator
but it's not enough
we need hope as well

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대조(antithesis) + 격언적 마무리(epigrammatic close) — "Fear ... hope"의 대비로 에세이의 존재 이유를 한 줄로 요약
